issues: 1x silhouette nearly unreadable — collapses to featureless blob
scattered outer particles create mushy/indistinct edge at all scales
light-blue ring particles lose contrast on light background
no strong outer silhouette shape — burst outline is irregular and soft
reasoning: The 4x renders show genuine charm: a central star motif surrounded by a cyan particle ring and outer orange sparks gives this explosion real personality and a toy-like, friendly feel — the star is the standout detail. Color palette (gold, cyan, orange) is warm and sticker-worthy. However, charm is undermined by silhouette clarity failures. At 1x the sprite is an unreadable dot — no star, no ring, just a small smear. At 4x the scattered pixel particles around the perimeter create a fuzzy, indistinct outer edge rather than a clean readable burst silhouette; the shape reads as 'blob with stuff around it' rather than a crisp explosion icon. On the light background the blue particles nearly disappear, reducing the ring detail significantly. The result is charming-but-mushy: passes the personality test, fails the instant-readability and both-background tests. Needs either a stronger outer silhouette boundary or reduced particle scatter to push into pass territory.
